content.full_text:
Repayment of Loan to Candidate is not Reportable Income on
Statement of Economic Interests

A candidate for elective office, who also is an officeholder,
loans money to his campaign committee. When the committee repays
the loan to the candidate, was the candidate/officeholder received
reportable income pursuant to §82030?

At the opinion request meeting of June 12, 1978, staff
concluded that repayment of a loan under these circumstances
does not constitute reportable income to the candidate/office-
holder. Pursuant to the Willie Brown and Janet Gray Hays
opinions, the staff concluded that the loan and its repayment
were Chapter 4 rather than Chapter 7 transactions and should
be reported accordingly.

Payment under an insurance policy.


Payments under an insurance policy are specifically

excluded from the definition of income. Staff concluded that

a person who borrows money under the terms of his insurance

policy has received a payment under the insurance policy within

the meaning of this provision. This conclusion assumes that all

policyholders have the same right to borrow against their insurance

policies and that there are no special privileges being accorded

the public official.

Section 82030(a)

Loans and Payment of Debt


The filer has received income if he receives a loan,

or repayment for a loan, which he has made to another.

sections.question: A candidate for elective office, who also is an officeholder, loans money to his campaign committee. When the committee repays the loan to the candidate, has the candidate/officeholder received reportable income pursuant to §82030?
sections.conclusion: At the opinion request meeting of June 12, 1978, staff concluded that repayment of a loan under these circumstances does not constitute reportable income to the candidate/officeholder. Pursuant to the Willie Brown and Janet Gray Hays opinions, the staff concluded that the loan and its repayment were Chapter 4 rather than Chapter 7 transactions and should be reported accordingly.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether repayment of a loan from a campaign committee to a candidate/officeholder constitutes reportable income under §82030.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Repayment of a loan from a campaign committee to a candidate/officeholder does not constitute reportable income; such transactions are Chapter 4 rather than Chapter 7 transactions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal section headers but contains clear Q/A structure. Multiple related topics addressed including insurance policy payments and loan/debt principles. Staff conclusion dated June 12, 1978. References Willie Brown and Janet Gray Hays opinions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A candidate for elective office, who also is an officeholder, loans money to his campaign committee. When the committee repays the loan to the candidate, has the candidate/officeholder received reportable income pursuant to §82030?

CONCLUSION: At the opinion request meeting of June 12, 1978, staff concluded that repayment of a loan under these circumstances does not constitute reportable income to the candidate/officeholder. Pursuant to the Willie Brown and Janet Gray Hays opinions, the staff concluded that the loan and its repayment were Chapter 4 rather than Chapter 7 transactions and should be reported accordingly.

When the committee repays the loan to the candidate, was the candidate/officeholder received reportable income pursuant to §82030? At the opinion request meeting of June 12, 1978, staff concluded that repayment of a loan under these circumstances does not constitute reportable income to the candidate/office- holder. Pursuant to the Willie Brown and Janet Gray Hays opinions, the staff concluded that the loan and its repayment were Chapter 4 rather than Chapter 7 transactions and should be reported accordingly. Payment under an insurance policy. Payments under an insurance policy are specifically excluded from the definition of income. Staff concluded that a person who borrows money under the terms of his insurance policy has received a payment under the insurance policy within the meaning of this provision. This conclusion assumes that all policyholders have the same right to borrow against their insurance policies and that there are no special privileges being accorded the public official. embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that repayment of a loan from a campaign committee to a candidate/officeholder is not reportable income under §82030. The letter also addresses treatment of insurance policy borrowing and general principles regarding loans and debt repayment.
